title: Volvo Autonomous Solutions and DSV announce autonomous freight operations in Texas, USA
author: Na
description: Volvo Autonomous Solutions (V.A.S.) and DSV have launched autonomous freight operations in Texas, beginning with commercial truckloads between Dallas and Houston using the Volvo VNL Autonomous integrated with Aurora Driver technology. The collaboration aims to improve safety, address driver shortages, and enable 24/7 operations while building experience for expansion to additional routes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: DSV is positioned as a market leader adopting cutting-edge autonomous technology to improve operational efficiency, address driver shortages, and enable 24/7 operations. This strategic partnership enhances their competitive positioning in logistics.

title: DSV, 1168 - INTERIM FINANCIAL REPORT Q1 2026
author: Dsv A/S
description: DSV Group reported EBIT before special items of DKK 4,855 million in Q1 2026, up 31.2% year-over-year, driven primarily by the Schenker acquisition integration. Despite geopolitical challenges in the Middle East impacting supply chains, the company maintained solid earnings and reiterated full-year 2026 EBIT guidance of DKK 23.0-25.5 billion. Schenker integration progressed strongly with over 50 countries integrated or undergoing integration, with DKK 9 billion in expected synergies by 2027.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 2026 financial performance with 31.2% EBIT growth year-over-year, successful Schenker integration progress across 50+ countries, maintained full-year guidance, and on-track synergy realization despite challenging market conditions and geopolitical headwinds. The company demonstrated resilience and operational execution.

title: Automotive Logistics Market to Surpass $386 Bn by 2031 as Transportation Segment Holds 58.34% Share, Reports Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global automotive logistics market is projected to grow from USD 282.90 billion in 2025 to USD 386.91 billion by 2031 at a CAGR of 5.36%. Growth is driven by rising electric vehicle demand, digitization, mobility-as-a-service expansion, and increased need for real-time supply chain transparency. Asia-Pacific is expected to be the fastest-growing region, while North America faces challenges from rising tariffs prompting near-shoring strategies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as key automotive logistics provider positioned to benefit from market growth, automation investments, and consolidation trends strengthening competitive positioning.

Listed among major automotive logistics companies well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand for specialized EV handling, temperature-controlled battery transport, and advanced digital solutions.

title: Mexico Freight and Logistics Market 2026-2031 - CEP Boom and Multimodal Expansion Propel Mexico's Logistics Sector Forward
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Mexico's freight and logistics market is projected to grow from USD 124.36 billion in 2025 to USD 170.39 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 5.39%. Growth is driven by near-shoring manufacturing relocation, e-commerce expansion, and multimodal infrastructure projects. However, cargo theft, regulatory compliance costs, and security challenges pose operational headwinds.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major global logistics operator positioned to benefit from Mexico's growing freight market, multimodal expansion, and cross-border trade volumes exceeding 25,000 monthly transactions.
Major courier and express operator positioned to benefit from sustained CEP volume growth, e-commerce expansion, and cross-border parcel demand in Mexico.
Leading parcel and logistics provider well-positioned to capitalize on CEP boom, e-commerce growth, and technology-enabled last-mile delivery solutions in Mexico.
Leading freight forwarding and logistics provider well-positioned to capture cross-border specialist market share driven by near-shoring and USMCA-enabled supply chain realignment.
Transportation and logistics provider with exposure to Mexico market growth, but limited specific mention of company-specific advantages or challenges in the report.
Global freight forwarder and contract logistics provider positioned to benefit from near-shoring trends, multimodal expansion, and integrated service demand in Mexico.
Major logistics provider positioned to benefit from cross-border manufacturing relocation, multimodal infrastructure projects, and growing demand for compliance-ready integrated services.

title: Air Freight Forwarding System Market Projected to Reach US$ 14.1 Billion by 2035 | Astute Analytica
author: Astute Analytica
description: The global air freight forwarding system market reached US$ 9.1 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow to US$ 14.1 billion by 2035 at a 4.4% CAGR. Global volumes hit 127 million tons in 2024 with 17 consecutive months of demand growth, driven by e-commerce surge, mega-mergers, and spot rates reaching US$ 5.12 per kg. Major players like Kuehne+Nagel and DSV are expanding capacity and investing in digital technologies to meet rising international logistics demands.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amazon Air is targeting a fleet of 100+ aircraft by early 2025 to gain more control over its supply chain, positioning itself to benefit from the 20-25% market share that e-commerce now represents in air freight volumes.

DSV demonstrated strong financial performance with 1.4 million tons of air cargo handled in 2024 (7.1% annual growth), air revenue surge of 9% to DKK 55.2 billion, Q4 volumes of 337,223 tons, and gross profits of DKK 11.9 billion with 27.3% operational conversion rates. The company also completed a major acquisition of DB Schenker for US$ 15.9 billion.

Lufthansa Cargo is modernizing its fleet with 89 weekly Boeing 777F freighter connections and recently increased by 7 weekly connections to accommodate demand, demonstrating strategic capacity expansion and operational efficiency improvements.
